description: The chart uses two different y-axes at vastly different scales: the left axis shows deaths in the hundreds of thousands (for India, Nigeria, China, South Africa), while the right axis shows Brazil's deaths in the low thousands — yet Brazil's line appears to visually track or intersect with the other countries' trends. A casual reader would likely assume all five lines share the same scale, dramatically overestimating Brazil's death toll relative to the others and perceiving a false similarity in their trajectories.
